description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined fuselage with a predominantly red and white color scheme, accented by blue and black details. The main rotor consists of two long, slender blades, while the tail section includes a horizontal stabilizer and a tail rotor for directional control. The cockpit is equipped with large, angular windows that provide wide visibility for the pilots, and the side doors are clearly marked with the number '276' and 'US COAST GUARD' insignia. The landing gear consists of sturdy black skids with crossbars for stability during ground contact. The helicopter's exterior is constructed from metal and composite materials, with a glossy finish that reflects light. The overall design suggests a utility or rescue role, suitable for urban or coastal operations.
description: A red and white helicopter with large cockpit windows, black landing skids, and prominent 'US COAST GUARD' markings. It features a two-blade main rotor and a tail rotor, designed for utility or rescue missions.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, including the main rotor, cockpit windows, landing skids, and '276' and 'US COAST GUARD' markings.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide cockpit windows, nose shape, and the alignment of the main rotor blades.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the tail section, main rotor, and the continuation of the red and white color scheme.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail rotor, tail boom, and the symmetry of the landing skids and stabilizers.
attribute_rotor_blades: Two long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored dark gray or black with no visible markings. Each blade spans nearly the full width of the helicopter.
attribute_tail_rotor: Tail rotor is present on the left side of the tail boom, featuring two short blades oriented vertically, colored dark gray or black, with no visible protective shroud.
attribute_landing_skids: Landing skids are black, tubular, and run parallel along the length of the fuselage, connected by crossbars for stability. They appear to be made of metal or composite material.
attribute_cockpit_windows: The cockpit has four large, angular windows: two front-facing and two side windows, all with black framing and a slight tint, providing wide visibility.
